node.html:
The web is for everyone—or at least, it should be. As digital experiences become more central to our lives, ensuring that websites are accessible to all users, including those with disabilities and neurodivergent individuals, is not just a legal requirement but a moral imperative. Yet, despite growing awareness, many web projects still fall short of true inclusivity. Why? Accessibility is complex, standards evolve, and manual audits are time-consuming and expensive.

Enter AI-powered automation: a new ally in the quest for accessible, user-friendly websites.

Why Automate Accessibility Audits?

Manual accessibility audits, while thorough, are resource-intensive and often only performed late in the development cycle. This can lead to:

  • Missed issues that impact real users
  • Costly rework
  • Frustration for both teams and users

Automating accessibility audits brings several benefits:

  • Continuous feedback: Issues are caught early and often, not just before launch.
  • Scalability: Large sites and frequent updates become manageable.
  • Empowerment: Developers, designers, and content creators get actionable insights without needing to be accessibility experts.

The Role of AI in Accessibility Audits

Traditional automated tools (like axe-core or Lighthouse) are great at catching obvious issues—missing alt text, color contrast failures, or improper heading structure. But they struggle with context, nuance, and the diversity of user needs.

Generative AI and large language models (LLMs) are changing the game. By understanding content, intent, and user journeys, AI can:

  • Flag ambiguous or misleading link text (e.g., “click here”)
  • Suggest more descriptive alt text for images
  • Detect cognitive overload in layouts or copy
  • Simulate diverse user experiences, including those of neurodivergent users

Examples: Tools Leveraging Generative AI

1. Microsoft Accessibility Insights + Copilot

Accessibility Insights for Web visualizes accessibility issues directly on your page.

Accessibility Insights for Web is a browser extension for Chrome and Edge that helps you test and fix accessibility issues in web applications. It now integrates with GitHub Copilot, providing AI-powered suggestions for fixing accessibility issues directly in your codebase. For example, Copilot can:

  • Recommend ARIA attributes (FastPass for Web)
  • Suggest semantic HTML changes
  • Explain why a pattern may be problematic for screen readers
  • Integrate with your workflow for continuous accessibility feedback (Get started)

What it’s used for:

  • Automated and guided accessibility testing
  • Visualizing and fixing issues in real time
  • Getting AI-powered code suggestions for accessibility

Tutorial: Accessibility Insights for Web Overview

2. Evinced

Evinced dashboard showing accessibility issues and recommendations.

Evinced uses AI to analyze web apps for accessibility barriers that traditional tools miss, such as dynamic content or custom widgets. Its engine leverages computer vision and natural language processing to:

  • Identify unlabeled controls
  • Flag confusing navigation flows
  • Generate human-readable remediation guidance

What it’s used for:

  • Detecting complex, dynamic, or custom accessibility issues
  • Providing actionable remediation steps
  • Integrating with CI/CD pipelines for continuous monitoring

Tutorial: Evinced Getting Started

3. Google Lighthouse + Gemini

Google Lighthouse accessibility audit results in Chrome DevTools.

Google Lighthouse is an open-source tool for auditing web page quality, including accessibility. When paired with Gemini, Google’s large language model, it can:

  • Summarize accessibility issues in plain English
  • Suggest improvements tailored to your site’s content and audience
  • Predict the impact of changes on real users

What it’s used for:

  • Automated accessibility scoring and reporting
  • Explaining issues and suggesting improvements with AI
  • Integrating with Chrome DevTools and CI/CD

Tutorial: Lighthouse Accessibility Audits

4. Axe by Deque

Axe DevTools browser extension highlights accessibility issues in the DOM.

Axe is a suite of accessibility testing tools built on the popular open-source axe-core library. It offers:

  • Axe DevTools: Browser extensions for automated and guided accessibility testing
  • Axe Auditor: Manual testing for full WCAG coverage
  • Axe Monitor: Continuous monitoring and reporting
  • Axe-core API: Integrate accessibility checks into your automated test suites

What it’s used for:

  • Automated and manual accessibility testing
  • Continuous monitoring and compliance
  • Integrating accessibility into development and CI/CD workflows

Tutorial: Axe DevTools Documentation

Example Workflow: AI-Driven Accessibility in Action

  1. Continuous Integration: Set up your CI pipeline to run automated accessibility checks on every pull request using tools like axe-core, Evinced, Accessibility Insights, or Lighthouse.
  2. AI Review: Use Copilot or Gemini to review flagged issues, generate code suggestions, and explain the impact. See Copilot coding agent with MCP.
  3. Team Collaboration: Share AI-generated reports with designers, developers, and content creators for holistic improvements.
  4. User Testing: Simulate diverse user journeys with AI to uncover edge cases and cognitive barriers.

Looking Ahead: The Human + AI Partnership

AI is not a silver bullet. Some issues—like the emotional tone of content or the lived experience of neurodivergent users—still require human empathy and expertise. But by automating the repetitive and technical aspects of accessibility, AI frees up teams to focus on what matters most: creating delightful, inclusive experiences for everyone.

Wrap-Up

The future of web accessibility is bright—and automated. By embracing AI-powered audits, we can build a web that’s not just compliant, but truly welcoming to all. As you explore these tools, remember: accessibility is a journey, not a checkbox. Let AI handle the heavy lifting, and let your team’s creativity and empathy shine.

Happy coding, and happy (inclusive) building!
